data.narrative:
Telexistence is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $170M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-sector, same-stage private companies (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat) alongside one relevant public reference point, spanning a wide range from $300M to $10.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $170M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $323M. Peer-median analysis across the six comparable companies produces a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics sub-segments, maturity, and commercialization progress within the cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $275M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $699M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables establish the upper boundary. Key uncertainties include the high dispersion within the peer set—particularly 1X Technologies' $10B valuation—and limited visibility into Telexistence's revenue scale relative to comparables. A refined range of $400M–$900M may be more defensible pending clarity on commercial traction and customer concentration.
